jobs.title: Desarrollador/a Full Stack Java APX Y ASO (TELETRABAJO)
jobs.description:
Sobre nosotros 
En Factum llevamos más de 15 años dando servicios de ciberseguridad, ciber inteligencia y servicios gestionados a más de 100 clientes. Nos caracterizamos por un buen ambiente de trabajo, una cultura orientada a la mejora continua y un fuerte compromiso con la innovación. 

Tu misión 
Buscamos un/a Desarrollador/a Full Stack Java con APX y ASO (IMPRESCINDIBLE), y experiencia en entornos bancarios, para incorporarse a un proyecto de larga duración vinculado a una importante entidad bancaria para uno de nuestros clientes. 
 
¿Qué harás en tu día a día? 
Desarrollo de aplicaciones Java para el sector bancario.  
Participación en el diseño, construcción y evolución de soluciones tecnológicas dentro del proyecto.  
Trabajo con tecnologías y entornos propios del ámbito bancario, incluyendo APX y ASO.  (IMPRESCINDIBLE) 
Colaboración con equipos multidisciplinares de tecnología, arquitectura, negocio y datos.  
Análisis y resolución de incidencias técnicas, aportando soluciones eficientes y orientadas a calidad.  
Participación en un entorno de trabajo colaborativo, con foco en mejora continua e innovación.  
 
¿Qué buscamos? 
Más de 2 años de experiencia profesional en desarrollo Java.  
Experiencia imprescindible de al menos 1 año trabajando con APX y ASO (IMPRESCINDIBLE).  
Titulación mínima: Ciclo Formativo de Grado Superior.  
Experiencia en desarrollo de aplicaciones dentro del sector bancario.  
Capacidad analítica y orientación a la resolución de problemas.  
Mentalidad de trabajo en equipo y capacidad para colaborar con equipos multidisciplinares.  
Se valorarán conocimientos o experiencia en Inteligencia Artificial.  
 
Condiciones 
Contrato indefinido.  
Modalidad: 100% teletrabajo, con alguna visita puntual a sede de cliente en Madrid Norte.  
Horario: 9:00 a 18:00, con flexibilidad de entrada y salida.  
Guardias rotatorias.  

¿Por qué Factum? 
Empresa consolidada, joven y con apetito para seguir creciendo y mejorando, podrás tener una carrera profesional. 
Seguro médico privado, tarjeta restaurante, tarjeta de transporte y cheques guardería mediante el esquema de retribución flexible. 
 
¡Estamos deseando conocerte!
jobs.tailored_advice: Dado que el uso de APX y ASO es un requisito excluyente, destaca en la parte superior de tu CV proyectos específicos donde hayas utilizado estas tecnologías bancarias. Si tu experiencia en banca es limitada, enfatiza tu capacidad de análisis y resolución de problemas complejos. Dado que el puesto es 100% teletrabajo, resalta tu autonomía y experiencia previa en entornos remotos. Si tienes conocimientos en IA, menciónalos explícitamente, ya que la oferta los valora como un plus.
jobs.cover_letter:
El sector bancario exige una precisión técnica y una capacidad de resolución de problemas que he cultivado tanto en mi formación académica como en mis proyectos personales. Conozco la relevancia de trabajar con tecnologías específicas como APX y ASO para garantizar la robustez de las aplicaciones, y estoy muy motivado por la oportunidad de aplicar mis conocimientos en Java en un entorno de alta exigencia como el de Factum.

Mi perfil combina una base sólida en desarrollo de software con una mentalidad orientada a la innovación. He desarrollado aplicaciones completas, desde la interfaz hasta la lógica de backend y bases de datos, integrando incluso soluciones de inteligencia artificial. Esta experiencia me ha permitido desarrollar una gran autonomía y una capacidad de adaptación rápida a nuevos entornos tecnológicos, cualidades que considero fundamentales para integrarme con éxito en vuestro equipo multidisciplinar.

Me entusiasma la posibilidad de contribuir a proyectos de larga duración en el sector bancario, aportando mi capacidad analítica y mi compromiso con la calidad del código. Estoy convencido de que mi proactividad y mi enfoque en la mejora continua encajan perfectamente con la cultura de Factum. Agradezco de antemano el tiempo dedicado a revisar mi perfil y quedo a vuestra entera disposición para concertar una entrevista y profundizar en cómo mi experiencia puede aportar valor a vuestros objetivos actuales.
jobs.cv_summary: Desarrollador de software con sólida base en Java y experiencia en desarrollo full-stack. Enfocado en la creación de soluciones robustas, con capacidad analítica para entornos bancarios y gran interés en la integración de tecnologías APX y ASO. Perfil proactivo, con nivel de inglés C1 y experiencia práctica en proyectos de IA.

jobs.title: Senior developer
jobs.location: Las Palmas de Gran Canaria, España
jobs.description:
En BITBOX nos encanta cuidar de nuestra plantilla y ahora estás a un paso de unirte a nosotros. ¿A qué esperas?
Tu misión va a ser:
Participar activamente en los proyectos, diseñando y programando los sistemas informáticos de acuerdo con los objetivos establecidos en el departamento. Responsable del código y del impacto técnico del producto.
Tus funciones:
Desarrollo técnico
Programar en los lenguajes requeridos en los proyectos.
Realizar pruebas y validaciones necesarias que aseguren el funcionamiento del sistema, corrigiendo incidencias de cualquier tipo si las hubiese.
Realizar el mantenimiento de las aplicaciones, corrigiendo cualquier punto que surja e introduciendo las modificaciones y cambios que requieren los clientes.
Diseñar arquitecturas y soluciones complejas.
Definir estándares de código limpio y mantenible, testing y despliegue.
Tomar decisiones técnicas clave para el proyecto en cuanto a rendimiento, escalabilidad y sostenibilidad.
Participar activamente en reviews con analistas, recibiendo y dando feedback.
Trabajar con bases de datos y APIs de forma consistente.
Optimizar sistemas existentes refactorizando y minimizando la deuda técnica.
Anticiparse a posibles problemas antes de que ocurran.
Análisis y diseño
Analizar, diseñar y desarrollar las nuevas funcionalidades del ERP de acuerdo con los requisitos y especificaciones marcadas por el responsable del área.
Descomponer tareas complejas en porciones claras.
Estimar tareas con cierta precisión.
Analizar el impacto funcional y técnico de los desarrollos o.
Colaborar con analistas de negocio para definir alcance del producto y prioridades.
Diseñar soluciones robustas, escalables y mantenibles
Evaluar riesgos técnicos y proponer alternativas.
Optimizar consultas y procesos de alto rendimiento.
Documentación y Formación
Redactar documentación técnica y manuales de soporte de los proyectos que se van a ejecutar y mantenerlos actualizados.
Enseñanza a developers junior y mid actuando como guía y referencia del equipo para cualquier decisión técnica contribuyendo a la mejora continua de la arquitectura y las buenas prácticas de desarrollo .
Qué buscamos en ti:
Experiencia en la mayoría de las siguientes tecnologías:

Backend 
* Java 
* Spring Boot 
* Spring Framework 
* JPA / Hibernate u otros ORM 
* REST APIs 
* Servicios web

Frontend 
* React 
* JavaScript / TypeScript 
* HTML5 
* CSS3

Bases de datos 
* PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL Server o MySQL 
* SQL avanzado 
* Optimización de consultas 
* Diseño de modelos de datos

Herramientas 
* Git 
* Docker (valorable) 
* CI/CD 
* Jira o herramientas similares 

Valoraremos especialmente 
Experiencia en desarrollo de ERP o software de gestión empresarial. 
Conocimientos de retail, logística, almacenes o facturación. 
Experiencia en aplicaciones con alta carga transaccional. 
Capacidad para analizar problemas complejos y proponer soluciones. 
Buenas prácticas de diseño y calidad del software. 

Buscamos personas que: 
Disfruten resolviendo problemas complejos. 
Quieran entender el negocio además de escribir código. 
Sean autónomas y proactivas. 
Aporten ideas para mejorar el producto y la arquitectura. 
Tengan orientación a la calidad y al trabajo en equipo.
jobs.tailored_advice: Dado que el puesto es Senior y se enfoca en un ERP, destaca en tu CV proyectos donde hayas gestionado alta carga transaccional y optimización de bases de datos SQL. Es crucial que menciones tu experiencia liderando o mentorizando a otros desarrolladores, ya que la oferta lo solicita explícitamente. Si tienes experiencia en el sector retail o logística, ponlo en la parte superior de tu perfil. Asegúrate de incluir ejemplos concretos de cómo has aplicado 'código limpio' (Clean Code) y cómo has reducido la deuda técnica en tus anteriores roles, ya que son pilares fundamentales para esta posición.
jobs.cover_letter:
La propuesta técnica de BITBOX para el desarrollo de soluciones robustas y escalables resuena profundamente con mi enfoque actual. Como desarrollador apasionado por la creación de software integral, he dedicado mi trayectoria a construir aplicaciones que no solo funcionan, sino que resuelven problemas reales, como mi plataforma de comercio electrónico con integración de IA o mis herramientas de gestión de datos. Mi base técnica en Java y JavaScript, junto con mi capacidad para gestionar el ciclo de vida completo de un proyecto, me permite abordar los retos de arquitectura y mantenimiento que requiere vuestro ERP.

Aunque mi perfil se encuentra en una etapa de crecimiento, mi capacidad de aprendizaje autodidacta y mi experiencia práctica con tecnologías clave como Java y el despliegue de aplicaciones me han permitido entregar soluciones en producción de manera autónoma. Entiendo la importancia de la calidad del código, la optimización de consultas y la creación de sistemas mantenibles, principios que aplico rigurosamente en cada uno de mis desarrollos personales.

Me entusiasma la posibilidad de aportar mi proactividad y mi visión técnica al equipo de BITBOX, contribuyendo a la mejora continua de vuestros productos. Estoy convencido de que mi adaptabilidad y mi enfoque orientado a resultados me permitirán integrarme rápidamente y sumar valor desde el primer día. Me encantaría tener la oportunidad de conversar sobre cómo mi experiencia y mis ganas de crecer pueden alinearse con los objetivos técnicos de vuestra compañía.

jobs.title: Desarrollador/a Full Stack .NET (C# / Angular)
jobs.description:
Desde MADIC Group, grupo internacional con más de 50 años de experiencia en soluciones para estaciones de servicio y movilidad, buscamos un/a Desarrollador/a Full Stack para nuestra filial Aseproda en Sevilla.
Tu rol será clave en el ciclo completo de vida de nuestras aplicaciones, participando tanto en el desarrollo de potentes lógicas de Backend como en la creación de interfaces de usuario modernas y funcionales (Web y Escritorio).
Funciones principales:
Desarrollo y mantenimiento evolutivo de aplicaciones de escritorio (WinForms, WPF) y entornos Web (ASP.NET MVC, Web APIs).
Implementación de interfaces de usuario dinámicas utilizando tecnologías Frontend modernas.
Diseño y optimización de bases de datos SQL (procedimientos, triggers y funciones).
Integración de servicios mediante APIs (REST, SOAP) y comunicación por Sockets.
Participación en la arquitectura de software bajo principios de código limpio y escalabilidad.
Requisitos técnicos:
Stack Backend: Experiencia sólida en .NET (C#), trabajando tanto con .NET Framework como con .NET Core.
Stack Frontend: Dominio de tecnologías web: Angular, TypeScript, JavaScript, HTML5 y CSS3.
Bases de Datos: Experiencia avanzada en SQL Server u otros entornos relacionales (PostgreSQL, MySQL).
Arquitectura y Herramientas: Conocimiento de patrones de diseño, inyección de dependencias y uso fluido de Git para control de versiones.
Inglés técnico para lectura de documentación.
Se valorará positivamente:
Experiencia con ORMs (Entity Framework, Dapper).
Conocimiento de entornos Cloud (Azure DevOps) y contenedores (Docker).
Experiencia en el sector de la energía o estaciones de servicio.
¿Qué ofrecemos?
Incorporación a un equipo técnico consolidado en una empresa líder en su sector.
Proyecto estable con tecnologías punteras y posibilidad de crecimiento profesional.
Entorno de trabajo dinámico en nuestra sede de Sevilla.
jobs.tailored_advice: Resalta tu experiencia en aplicaciones de escritorio (WinForms/WPF) junto a tus habilidades en Angular, ya que es un perfil híbrido poco común. Si has trabajado con protocolos de comunicación o Sockets, inclúyelo explícitamente. Asegúrate de mencionar proyectos donde hayas optimizado consultas SQL complejas o procedimientos almacenados, pues es un requisito clave para Aseproda. Si tienes experiencia en el sector industrial o energético, destácalo en tu perfil profesional.

jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer, Product (Belo Horizonte)
jobs.description:
This is a remote role for candidates located in Belo Horizonte, Brazil. 

About LawnStarter

LawnStarter is the nation's leading on-demand marketplace for lawn care and outdoor services, with over $100M in annual bookings. We're expanding beyond lawn care to become the one-stop shop for all home services — operating across three brands (LawnStarter, Lawn Love, Home Gnome) on a single shared platform.

About Engineering at LawnStarter

We build in small, focused initiative teams: a Product Engineer working alongside a PM and a designer, supported by an Engineering Manager who helps you grow. You'll also work shoulder-to-shoulder with engineering peers across initiatives in a shared codebase. The whole team owns whether the work moves its metric.

AI coding agents are a force multiplier here — they give a small, senior team the leverage to ship more, faster, and at a higher bar for quality. We hire engineers who are wired for ownership and energized by shipping to a real marketplace with customers and pros on both sides.

 

The Role

You're the engineering anchor of an initiative — working as part of a tight team with your PM and designer, and alongside engineering peers on adjacent initiatives. You have a hand in the full lifecycle: shaping the problem, deciding the technical approach, directing AI agents to implement much of the code, shipping to production, and — with your team — owning the outcome.

You're measured by impact, not by lines of code merged. When an agent can ship something safely, your job is to make sure it's done right and the metric moves. When the work calls for careful, hand-written code in a sensitive area, you write it yourself.

What makes this role exciting:

  • You ship end-to-end. From problem-framing through production to the post-launch metric review — you see the whole arc and own the result with your team.
  • You work as a true product partner. You sit at the table with PM and design, bringing engineering judgment to product calls and product sense to engineering calls.
  • You get real autonomy — with the right checkpoints. You make most technical calls yourself, with architect review on significant architectural decisions and fast input from peers.
  • You operate at a staff bar. You're trusted to make the call, ship the hard thing, and stand behind the outcome.

 

What You'll Own

 

  • The technical approach — architecture, data model, integration choices, rollout plan, observability, and rollback strategy for your initiative. You make most calls yourself and bring significant architectural decisions to architect review; you document them, and revisit if the data says you were wrong.
  • Implementation quality — the prompts, guardrails, evals, tests, and review loop that let agents ship safe, correct, production-ready code. Most lines will be agent-authored, and you're accountable for them — held to the same standard as the rest of the team working in a shared codebase.
  • Cross-functional partnership — daily working contact with your PM (scope, tradeoffs) and designer (UX decisions, in-tool prototyping), regular collaboration with engineering peers, and weekly check-ins with your EM.
  • The initiative outcome — the metric the initiative was set up to move. With your PM, you present results 2–4 weeks post-launch and share the "did it work" answer.
  • A high bar for what ships — production correctness, security, performance, observability, and the experience for customers and pros. Agents accelerate you; they don't lower the bar.

 

Problems to Solve

 

Leading AI agents at a staff-level quality bar Most of the code on your initiative will be authored by AI agents. The craft is making them ship as if a senior engineer wrote it: prompts that encode our conventions, evals that catch issues before merge, tests that exercise the edges, observability that catches a regression before a customer does. How do you build a workflow that lets a small team ship far more than its size would suggest?

Owning decisions with high autonomy You have real latitude to make and document technical calls quickly — with architect review on the big architectural ones and peers to pressure-test your thinking. How do you move fast, keep your team aligned, and stay accountable to the outcome?

Shipping outcomes, not features Each initiative is measured by a metric — a conversion rate, a retention curve, a pro-funnel KPI, a unit-economics shift. You're accountable for the number alongside your team. How do you scope to actually move it, decide what *not* to build, and have the discipline to follow up 2–4 weeks after launch?

 

What Success Looks Like (Year 1)

 

  • Initiative outcomes hit — You've shipped 3–4 initiatives end-to-end, and at least two clearly moved their metric (with the post-launch review to prove it).
  • Agent workflow that travels — The prompts, evals, and review loop you built are picked up by peers on other initiatives.
  • Faster cycle time — Median time from problem-framing to first production rollout on your initiatives is meaningfully shorter.
  • Quality holds — No customer- or pro-facing regression traceable to agent-authored code that slipped through your review.
  • Visible leverage — Peers point to artifacts you left behind — runbooks, evals, agent workflows, post-launch write-ups — as references they use.



Requirements

Who You Are

 

  • AI-native. Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, or equivalent are how you ship today — daily, on production work. You have real opinions about prompts, evals, agent loops, and review workflows, and you know when to let the agent run versus write it yourself.
  • Operating at a lead level. Whatever your current title, you've been the person making the call, shipping the hard thing, and standing behind whether it worked.
  • Outcome-driven. You measure your week in "did the metric move" and "did the experience get better." You read the post-launch dashboard and own the answer.
  • A strong horizontal partner. You hold your own with a strong PM and designer, and you collaborate well with engineering peers in a shared codebase. You bring engineering judgment to product calls and product judgment to engineering calls.
  • Decisive and documented. You make architecture, data-model, and rollout calls, write them down, get fast input, and move.
  • A force multiplier. Your impact compounds beyond your own initiative because you leave reusable artifacts behind — agent workflows, evals, runbooks, post-launch reviews.
  • Customer- and pro-minded. This is a real marketplace with real people on both sides, and you care about the outcomes for both.

 

Good to Know

 

  • An individual-contributor role with room to grow. People management sits with the EM — but the path into management is an open door for those who want it.
  • A product-engineering role, end-to-end. You ship features that move metrics; platform and architecture work happen inside the initiative when the outcome needs them.
  • Hands-on, with a high quality bar. Agents handle much of the implementation; you bring the judgment, design, safety, and accountability. The bar is high.
  • Shipping to a live marketplace. With $100M+ in bookings, customers and pros use what you ship within the same week.

 

Tech You'll Touch

 

  • AI agents — Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, internal agent stack, MCP servers, evals tooling
  • Backend — PHP/Laravel
  • Frontend — TypeScript/React/React Native (customer & pro apps, web and mobile)
  • Data — Redshift, dbt, Segment, Airflow
  • Infra — AWS, Datadog, Sentry, GitHub Actions
  • Documentation & process — Brain (Claude Code skills + docs repo), Confluence, Jira

You don't need every box checked. You need deep skill in at least one of our stacks plus credible production experience with AI coding agents.



Benefits

  • Competitive salary of USD $80,000–$100,000 annual base
  • Work from anywhere
  • High ownership and autonomy
  • Fast-moving team that loves to build, learn, and grow
jobs.tailored_advice: Highlight your 'AI-native' workflow immediately in your summary; explicitly mention your experience using Cursor or Claude Code to ship production-ready features. Since this is a Staff-level role, focus your bullet points on 'outcomes' and 'metrics' rather than just tasks—use the STAR method to describe how you moved a specific KPI (e.g., conversion rate or retention) through a technical initiative. Emphasize your ability to act as a product partner by detailing how you've collaborated with PMs and designers to shape product strategy. Finally, showcase your 'force multiplier' capability by mentioning any documentation, runbooks, or automated workflows you've created that helped your team scale.

jobs.title: Frontend Developer
jobs.description:
(All applications will be considered ONLY through Wellfound. Please do not contact any employee of the company as no such contact/connection will be considered as an application for the job.)

This posting is for a Frontend Developer, you would work alongside our team. We are a fully remote team so it does not matter which country or time zone you are in, as long as you are available from 10 AM to 1 PM (EST). We are NOT looking for part-time work, contractors, software development companies or consultants, so please only apply if you're looking for a full-time position.

Quinncia is a career readiness platform that improves college student outcomes by improving students' resumes and interview skills using our AI. Quinncia allows career service to scale their offerings allowing each student to get personalized feedback on their resume and interviewing skills. With over 60% unemployment or underemployment rate recently graduated students, our goal is to integrate career readiness into the academic curriculum and make sure every student has a job within 90 days of graduation.

Projects you'll be working on:

We're focused on enhancing our UI and UX, as well as streamlining operations and improving our technical foundation. As a core member of the Front-End team, you will influence how we build individual features, take ownership of end to end product quality that will help make our app/website reliable We want you to focus on high-quality code and know that quality is more than just unit tests and code coverage. You would look for opportunities to automate, always package up reusable code, be constantly learning, ready to pick up new technologies, patterns, and paradigms.

COOL TECH

  • Chatbots
  • Computer vision
  • Data visualization
  • Machine learning
  • Natural language processing
  • Video processing/conferencing

Being a Frontend engineer you will need strong logical skills but also understand design patterns and ability to predict edge cases for handling different issues. You will be working with our lead designer but a good understanding of UI and UX would be needed.

Requirements

  • Front-end developer position (required tech):
  • Profound knowledge of Javascript
  • TypeScript
  • Next.js
  • Practical and recent experience with React.js
  • Experience communicating with RESTful services
  • Experience with testing (e.g. Jest)
  • Experience with bundling of Javascript code (e.g. Webpack)
  • Have deep knowledge in CSS, preprocessors (e.g. Sass)
  • GraphQL

Bonus skills:

  • Docker
  • Ramda

Whatever else you decide - we're constantly re-evaluating our stack and tools Having prior experience with the technologies is a plus, but not mandatory for skilled candidates.

PS: Please add your Github account link to your application, as it will make the evaluation process easier and more robust.
jobs.tailored_advice: Highlight your experience with Next.js and TypeScript prominently, as these are core requirements. Since the company values UI/UX and product quality, include specific examples of how you have improved performance or user experience in past projects. Mention your familiarity with GraphQL and testing frameworks like Jest to show you can handle their full stack. Crucially, ensure your GitHub profile is clean, well-documented, and contains recent code samples, as they explicitly stated this is vital for their evaluation. Finally, emphasize your ability to work independently in a remote setting while maintaining the required 10 AM - 1 PM EST availability.
jobs.cover_letter: Building products that solve real-world problems is what drives me, and Quinncia’s mission to bridge the gap for recent graduates resonates deeply with my own experience as a developer. I have spent the last few years building full-stack applications from scratch, including an AI-powered e-commerce platform that integrates complex features like virtual assistants and automated notifications. My technical foundation is built on JavaScript, TypeScript, and React.js, and I am comfortable managing the entire lifecycle of a project, from UI/UX design to deployment. In my recent projects, I have utilized Next.js for performance and CSS for responsive design, ensuring that the end-user experience is both intuitive and reliable. I am also well-versed in testing with Jest and managing build processes with Webpack, which allows me to maintain high code quality and scalability. I am particularly excited about the opportunity to contribute to your frontend team, as I enjoy the challenge of optimizing UI/UX and implementing complex features like data visualization and AI integration. My background in engineering and my hands-on approach to development mean I am always ready to learn new paradigms and automate workflows to improve efficiency. I would love to discuss how my technical skills and passion for building impactful software can contribute to the continued success of Quinncia. Are you available for a brief conversation next week to explore how I can support your team?
jobs.cv_summary: Full-stack developer with a strong focus on React.js, Next.js, and TypeScript. Experienced in building and deploying AI-integrated web applications with a deep understanding of CSS and RESTful services. Passionate about writing clean, testable code and optimizing UI/UX to deliver high-quality, scalable software solutions.

jobs.title: Senior Quality Engineer (Belo Horizonte)
jobs.description:
This is a remote role for candidates located in Belo Horizonte, Brazil

 

About LawnStarter

LawnStarter is the nation's leading on-demand marketplace for lawn care and outdoor services, with over $100M in annual bookings. We're expanding beyond lawn care to become the one-stop shop for all home services, operating across three brands (LawnStarter, Lawn Love, Home Gnome) on a single shared platform.

About Engineering at LawnStarter

We've spent the last two years rebuilding the platform from the ground up: new customer app, new Pro onboarding, job distribution engine, and data infrastructure. The foundation is solid. Now we're moving into expanded service verticals, dynamic pricing, and AI-powered features at scale. Quality needs to keep up.

Our stack is PHP (Laravel) and React/React Native, with Cypress as our primary test framework. We actively use Claude Code as part of our engineering workflow, not as an experiment, but as a core part of how we work.

 

The Role

As we scale into new service verticals and grow the engineering team, quality can't be reactive. This role exists to make quality proactive: owning the automation coverage, shaping the bug process, and partnering closely with Product, Design, and the Data team to catch problems before they reach customers.

You'll report to the Engineering Manager and be embedded across the engineering organization. This is hands-on and strategic, helping raise the quality ceiling for the whole team.

What makes this role different:

  • Cross-functional influence: You work directly with engineers, PMs, Designers, and the Data team to surface risks early, understand requirements deeply, and propose better solutions. You may be embedded in one or two delivery teams.
  • Automation ownership: You own the strategy for building and expanding our automated test coverage, not just executing test cases someone else wrote.
  • Process shaper: You'll identify gaps in our quality process and have the authority to fix them, not just flag them.

 

What You'll Own

  • Automated test coverage: Design and grow our E2E and integration test suite using Cypress. Set the strategy, prioritize coverage gaps, and raise the floor across the platform.
  • Bug process: Own the full lifecycle from triage to production fix. Keep it moving, keep stakeholders informed, and make sure nothing falls through the cracks.
  • Cross-functional quality partnership: Collaborate with PMs, Designers, and the Data team to review requirements, raise risks early, and define what "done" actually means.
  • Process improvement: Identify weaknesses in how we deliver software and propose solutions. Not just document them.
  • Exploratory testing: Test new features and existing flows on web and mobile across devices, catching what automation misses.

 

Problems to Solve

Keeping automation coverage sharp as the product evolves Our E2E coverage is solid. The challenge is staying ahead: bringing new ideas, exploring different types of coverage, and finding smarter ways to automate different aspects of the application as the product grows and new service verticals come online.

Exploring new tools and leveraging AI to improve quality operations AI is changing how we build software, and it's changing how we can test it too. There's real opportunity here to use AI tools to increase output quality, cut repetitive work, and discover coverage gaps we'd otherwise miss. We want someone who actively explores that space, not someone who waits to be told what to use.

 

What Success Looks Like (Year 1)

  • Automation coverage stays strong: New features ship with automated tests as a baseline, and coverage strategies evolve with the product.
  • Bug process runs smoothly: Triage to fix cycle is predictable and stakeholders know where things stand without having to ask.
  • Deep business knowledge: You understand how the marketplace works well enough to anticipate edge cases on your own.
  • Quality process improvement shipped: At least one meaningful improvement to how we deliver quality is in place and being used by the team.



Requirements

Who You Are

AI-native. You use AI tools like Claude Code to write, maintain, and extend test code, not as a novelty, but as a standard part of how you work. You're comfortable with browser MCPs, agent workflows, and building scripts that automate the boring parts of QA. This is unlikely to be a good fit if you're skeptical of AI in the engineering workflow.

Automation-first. Your default when you find a problem is to ask how to prevent it automatically. You build test infrastructure that outlasts any individual test case. This is unlikely to be a good fit if your strength is manual testing and automation is something you tolerate rather than drive.

Customer-obsessed. You think like a user. When you're testing a flow, you're asking whether it actually works for a real customer, not just whether it matches the spec. This is unlikely to be a good fit if you treat quality as a checkbox rather than a standard.

Adaptable. You don't get attached to a single tool, stack, or process. We're in a period of rapid evolution in how software gets built and tested, especially with AI, and you lean into that rather than resist it. This is unlikely to be a good fit if you thrive only in stable, well-defined tooling environments.

Collaborative and direct. You work across Product, Design, Engineering, and Operations. You surface risks early, speak plainly about what you find, and push back when something isn't ready to ship. This is unlikely to be a good fit if you prefer to work in isolation or soften feedback to avoid friction.

Technically solid. You're comfortable reading and writing code. You understand front and backend web services well enough to test them properly, write meaningful SQL queries, and debug issues across the stack. This is unlikely to be a good fit if your technical depth is limited to a single layer or framework.

 

This Role Is NOT

  • A manual-testing role: Most of your testing work should be building automation, not executing test scripts by hand.
  • A ticket-executor role: You're expected to shape what gets worked on and how, not just process a backlog.
  • A single-tool role: We use Cypress today, but we adapt. If you're only comfortable in one framework or one part of the stack, this will be limiting.
  • A role for someone who avoids ambiguity: The business is changing fast. New verticals, new flows, new edge cases. You need to be comfortable moving before everything is defined.
  • A back-of-the-line role: Quality is a first-class concern here. You're expected to have opinions and influence the process upstream, not just verify what's already been built.



Benefits

Compensation & Benefits

  • Base salary: $45-$65k
  • Equity: We want you invested in the quality and long-term health of the platform you're helping build.
  • Fully remote: Work from anywhere in the Americas. Deep focus and async collaboration are the norm here.
  • Unlimited PTO: We focus on results. Take what you need.

 

LawnStarter provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or genetics. We comply with applicable state and local laws governing nondiscrimination in employment.
jobs.tailored_advice: To stand out for this role, emphasize your 'AI-native' mindset in your CV. Explicitly list projects where you used AI agents (like Claude Code or similar) to accelerate test automation or debug complex flows. Since they value 'Automation-first' engineers, highlight your experience building test infrastructure rather than just writing individual test cases. Mention your proficiency with Cypress and your ability to write SQL queries for data validation. Finally, frame your experience in terms of 'cross-functional influence'—show how you have proactively identified risks and improved quality processes in previous roles, rather than just acting as a ticket executor.

My experience with PHP, Laravel, and React allows me to contribute immediately to your existing stack, while my hands-on work with automation ensures that quality is built into the product lifecycle rather than treated as an afterthought.

I am particularly drawn to your "AI-native" engineering culture. jobs.title: Senior Product Engineer (Fullstack)
jobs.location: Europe, UK, Germany, France, European timezones
jobs.description:
About Clipster

Clipster is where brands and creators connect to turn views into profits. Clippers (Creators) clip, remix, and post branded content on TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, X, and beyond — earning money for every view they deliver. Brands get a significantly better influencer marketing experience thanks to scale, simplicity, and reliable performance tracking.

We started as an influencer marketing agency. While clients loved our results, delivering them was a pain. So we started automating everything we did — and it paid off.

2025 was a great year for us:

  • We launched our platform in early 2025
  • We grew our team to 35 members
  • We scaled from 0 to more than 100,000 creators and paid out more than $5M to them
  • We acquired more than 120 clients solely through word of mouth. Our growth has been 100% organic
  • We ran campaigns for companies such as Telegram, UFC, Universal Music Group and artists such as Elton John, Ed Sheeran, Playboi Carti, and Kendrick Lamar
  • We're profitable and have secured our first investor

The role

We are looking for a Backend-Heavy Senior Engineer who isn't afraid to touch the full stack. While your primary domain will be our Golang services and distributed architecture, you are a "Product Engineer" at heart — meaning you understand the "why" behind the code and can jump into the React/Next.js frontend whenever it's the shortest path to delivering value.

The mindset we hire for

  • Intensity & Grit — You run toward hard problems and stay until they are solved.
  • Ambitious Simplification — You have the intelligence to solve complex problems and the wisdom to choose the simplest solution.
  • Ownership & Mission Alignment — You don't "close tickets"; you ship outcomes. You care about the creator economy as much as we do.
  • AI Readiness — You leverage modern tools to multiply your output and keep us at the cutting edge.
  • Humility & Collaboration — You are a high-bandwidth communicator who mentors others and leaves your ego at the door.

Your technical toolkit

  • Core Backend — 5+ years of experience, with at least 3+ years of mastery in Golang. Comfortable with high-performance distributed systems.
  • Fullstack Versatility — Backend is your home, but you have the skills to navigate and contribute to a React/Next.js frontend.
  • Infrastructure & Data — Deep knowledge of PostgreSQL, Redis, and ideally GCP.
  • Architectural Mindset — You move seamlessly from system design to shipping production-ready code. Clean code, test coverage, and documentation are your tools for maintaining velocity.

Bonus points

  • Experience with payment systems (we handle around $1M in monthly payouts).
  • Event-driven architectures and real-time data processing.

Why Clipster

  • Total ownership - Join a lean tech team of fewer than 15 people. What you build today, millions will use tomorrow.
  • Remote & async - Work where you are most effective. We only ask for availability during our core hours (10am – 2pm UTC+2).
  • Zero bureaucracy - No endless meetings. We value documentation, Slack, and Loom for high-bandwidth, low-noise collaboration.

Next steps

  • Discovery call - a brief chat with our recruiter.
  • Pair programming - a hands-on session with one of our engineers.
  • System design - a discussion about an architectural problem, based on our day-to-day problems.
  • Engineering excellence discussion.
  • Final meet - chat with our Founder and PM to talk mission and values.
jobs.tailored_advice: To stand out, emphasize your 'Product Engineer' mindset by highlighting projects where you owned the full lifecycle, not just backend tickets. Since they value 'Ambitious Simplification,' describe a specific instance where you chose a simpler architectural path over a complex one to increase delivery speed. Explicitly mention your experience with high-concurrency systems and any work with payment processing or event-driven architectures, as these are key bonus points. Finally, demonstrate your 'AI Readiness' by mentioning specific tools or workflows you use to automate testing, documentation, or code generation to maintain high velocity.
jobs.cover_letter:
Clipster’s mission to bridge the gap between creators and brands through automation resonates deeply with my own approach to software development. I have spent the last few years building production-ready applications, such as my AI-integrated e-commerce platform, where I managed everything from the frontend interface to the backend logic. I thrive on the challenge of turning complex requirements into simple, scalable code, and I am eager to bring that same intensity to your engineering team.

My technical background is built on a foundation of full-stack versatility. While I am constantly refining my backend architecture skills, I am equally comfortable navigating React and Next.js to deliver immediate value. I have a strong grasp of PostgreSQL and Redis for data management, and I am highly motivated to apply these tools within a high-performance GCP environment to support Clipster’s rapid growth.

I admire the lean, ownership-driven culture you have cultivated. I am not just looking to ship features; I want to contribute to the long-term success of a platform that is already making a massive impact in the creator economy. I am ready to dive into your distributed systems and help maintain the high velocity that has defined your organic growth this year.

I would love to discuss how my technical toolkit and passion for building efficient, user-centric software align with the challenges your team is currently solving. Are you available for a brief call next week to explore this further?

jobs.title: Staff Product Engineer (Campinas)
jobs.description:
This is a remote role for candidates located in Campinas, Brazil.

 

About LawnStarter

LawnStarter is the nation's leading on-demand marketplace for lawn care and outdoor services, with over $100M in annual bookings. We're expanding beyond lawn care to become the one-stop shop for all home services — operating across three brands (LawnStarter, Lawn Love, Home Gnome) on a single shared platform.

About Engineering at LawnStarter

We build in small, focused initiative teams: a Product Engineer working alongside a PM and a designer, supported by an Engineering Manager who helps you grow. You'll also work shoulder-to-shoulder with engineering peers across initiatives in a shared codebase. The whole team owns whether the work moves its metric.

AI coding agents are a force multiplier here — they give a small, senior team the leverage to ship more, faster, and at a higher bar for quality. We hire engineers who are wired for ownership and energized by shipping to a real marketplace with customers and pros on both sides.

 

The Role

You're the engineering anchor of an initiative — working as part of a tight team with your PM and designer, and alongside engineering peers on adjacent initiatives. You have a hand in the full lifecycle: shaping the problem, deciding the technical approach, directing AI agents to implement much of the code, shipping to production, and — with your team — owning the outcome.

You're measured by impact, not by lines of code merged. When an agent can ship something safely, your job is to make sure it's done right and the metric moves. When the work calls for careful, hand-written code in a sensitive area, you write it yourself.

What makes this role exciting:

  • You ship end-to-end. From problem-framing through production to the post-launch metric review — you see the whole arc and own the result with your team.
  • You work as a true product partner. You sit at the table with PM and design, bringing engineering judgment to product calls and product sense to engineering calls.
  • You get real autonomy — with the right checkpoints. You make most technical calls yourself, with architect review on significant architectural decisions and fast input from peers.
  • You operate at a staff bar. You're trusted to make the call, ship the hard thing, and stand behind the outcome.

 

What You'll Own

 

  • The technical approach — architecture, data model, integration choices, rollout plan, observability, and rollback strategy for your initiative. You make most calls yourself and bring significant architectural decisions to architect review; you document them, and revisit if the data says you were wrong.
  • Implementation quality — the prompts, guardrails, evals, tests, and review loop that let agents ship safe, correct, production-ready code. Most lines will be agent-authored, and you're accountable for them — held to the same standard as the rest of the team working in a shared codebase.
  • Cross-functional partnership — daily working contact with your PM (scope, tradeoffs) and designer (UX decisions, in-tool prototyping), regular collaboration with engineering peers, and weekly check-ins with your EM.
  • The initiative outcome — the metric the initiative was set up to move. With your PM, you present results 2–4 weeks post-launch and share the "did it work" answer.
  • A high bar for what ships — production correctness, security, performance, observability, and the experience for customers and pros. Agents accelerate you; they don't lower the bar.

 

Problems to Solve

 

Leading AI agents at a staff-level quality bar Most of the code on your initiative will be authored by AI agents. The craft is making them ship as if a senior engineer wrote it: prompts that encode our conventions, evals that catch issues before merge, tests that exercise the edges, observability that catches a regression before a customer does. How do you build a workflow that lets a small team ship far more than its size would suggest?

Owning decisions with high autonomy You have real latitude to make and document technical calls quickly — with architect review on the big architectural ones and peers to pressure-test your thinking. How do you move fast, keep your team aligned, and stay accountable to the outcome?

Shipping outcomes, not features Each initiative is measured by a metric — a conversion rate, a retention curve, a pro-funnel KPI, a unit-economics shift. You're accountable for the number alongside your team. How do you scope to actually move it, decide what *not* to build, and have the discipline to follow up 2–4 weeks after launch?

 

What Success Looks Like (Year 1)

 

  • Initiative outcomes hit — You've shipped 3–4 initiatives end-to-end, and at least two clearly moved their metric (with the post-launch review to prove it).
  • Agent workflow that travels — The prompts, evals, and review loop you built are picked up by peers on other initiatives.
  • Faster cycle time — Median time from problem-framing to first production rollout on your initiatives is meaningfully shorter.
  • Quality holds — No customer- or pro-facing regression traceable to agent-authored code that slipped through your review.
  • Visible leverage — Peers point to artifacts you left behind — runbooks, evals, agent workflows, post-launch write-ups — as references they use.



Requirements

Who You Are

 

  • AI-native. Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, or equivalent are how you ship today — daily, on production work. You have real opinions about prompts, evals, agent loops, and review workflows, and you know when to let the agent run versus write it yourself.
  • Operating at a lead level. Whatever your current title, you've been the person making the call, shipping the hard thing, and standing behind whether it worked.
  • Outcome-driven. You measure your week in "did the metric move" and "did the experience get better." You read the post-launch dashboard and own the answer.
  • A strong horizontal partner. You hold your own with a strong PM and designer, and you collaborate well with engineering peers in a shared codebase. You bring engineering judgment to product calls and product judgment to engineering calls.
  • Decisive and documented. You make architecture, data-model, and rollout calls, write them down, get fast input, and move.
  • A force multiplier. Your impact compounds beyond your own initiative because you leave reusable artifacts behind — agent workflows, evals, runbooks, post-launch reviews.
  • Customer- and pro-minded. This is a real marketplace with real people on both sides, and you care about the outcomes for both.

 

Good to Know

 

  • An individual-contributor role with room to grow. People management sits with the EM — but the path into management is an open door for those who want it.
  • A product-engineering role, end-to-end. You ship features that move metrics; platform and architecture work happen inside the initiative when the outcome needs them.
  • Hands-on, with a high quality bar. Agents handle much of the implementation; you bring the judgment, design, safety, and accountability. The bar is high.
  • Shipping to a live marketplace. With $100M+ in bookings, customers and pros use what you ship within the same week.

 

Tech You'll Touch

 

  • AI agents — Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, internal agent stack, MCP servers, evals tooling
  • Backend — PHP/Laravel
  • Frontend — TypeScript/React/React Native (customer & pro apps, web and mobile)
  • Data — Redshift, dbt, Segment, Airflow
  • Infra — AWS, Datadog, Sentry, GitHub Actions
  • Documentation & process — Brain (Claude Code skills + docs repo), Confluence, Jira

You don't need every box checked. You need deep skill in at least one of our stacks plus credible production experience with AI coding agents.



Benefits

  • Competitive salary of USD $80,000–$100,000 annual base
  • Work from anywhere
  • High ownership and autonomy
  • Fast-moving team that loves to build, learn, and grow

jobs.title: Senior Independent Software Developer
jobs.description:
You must be located in the Americas, Europe, or Israel to apply.


A·Team is a VC-backed, stealth, application-only home on the internet for senior independent software builders to team up with hand-picked, high-growth companies on their next big thing. 

After talking with hundreds of independent engineers, designers, and product folks, we heard over and over that finding vetted, high-quality, consistent clients is hard, and projects are often too small to be rewarding. A·Team matches small teams of the most talented builders in the world with companies backed by a16z, YC, Softbank, General Catalyst, etc. on a contract basis for many of their most important initiatives. We quietly launched in May 2020, and have helped A·Teamers earn $85+ million since.

As part of A·Team, you can expect:

  • High-paying, meaningful missions with the most audacious companies sent your way; generally $90-$150+/hr, with vetted, fascinating clients doing work that matters. We're picky about who we partner with; new clients only come in via trusted referral. We've worked with Lyft, McGraw Hill, ClearCo, Pepsi, Walmart, the former CEO of Waze, the leading vaccine production software, several new unicorns we can't say here, and dozens of startups backed by a16z/YC/Softbank/Insight/Tiger/etc.
  • Work alongside friends old & new: our niche is small/diverse product teams, since clients with larger budgets and higher-impact work tell us they want teams, not individuals. Of course, we keep friends together whenever we can.
  • Full autonomy: say "no" to things that don't excite you. The most talented builders often juggle a few things at once, so there's never pressure to join an A·Team mission if you don't have the bandwidth. If we're no longer a fit, it's easy to leave or pause too. 
  • Small, curated, off-the-record gatherings: for conversations hard to have elsewhere. Long-term, we're creating micro-communities for the world's top builders to become friends around the things they care about.
  • Keep 100% of what you earn: if you charge $120/hr, you get $120/hr. A·Team makes money by charging a small, flat, transparent platform fee on top of your rate.

 

 

How to apply:

Go here: https://build.a.team/remotivereferral + mention Remotive. We respect your time so the application is short. We're also much more interested in seeing what you've made, and excited to chat more if there’s a fit.

What you’ll do:

  • Once part of A.Team, you’ll regularly be invited to impactful missions that match your interests. Find the right pick from early-stage incubations with world-class founders, to fast-growing super-funded companies, to old school non-tech incumbents looking to build as a tech giant would

  • Missions usually involve building an ambitious piece of software from 0 to 1 as part of a small 3-4 person team. 

  • You’ll be paid to scope it out, give the client options, guide strategy, and execute on the selected solution. Sometimes the client has a clear vision, sometimes not; which is why A.Team builders tend to be senior folks who can work together to find the right direction. 

Who A·Team is for:

  • Senior software developers who left large companies and high-growth startups to pursue their craft with autonomy.

  • Those who prefer consistent contract work over a full-time role, who want to create a variety of new products alongside other top-tier builders.

  • The majority of A.Teamers spend most of their time doing independent work, but a sizeable percentage are either employed full-time (but testing out client work), bootstrapping a side project, or looking for their next big thing

Who A·Team is not for:

  • People looking for small gigs

  • Folks looking to build simple wordpress/wix/squarespace-style websites

  • Those still early in their careers (<4 years) and recent university/bootcamp grads (at least not yet)

Our long-term vision:

A·Team is a new type of company for a new kind of independent software builder. We call them "unhirables": people who traditional companies couldn’t hire full-time even if they wanted to, but who want to do their most meaningful work with their favorite people in small, autonomous, distributed expert teams. 

To help us secure amazing missions, we raised $60 million+  from Insight Partenrs, Tiger Global, NFX, RocNation, along with the former CEO of Upwork, the founders of Fiverr and Lemonade, Apple's Global Head of Recruiting, YC Partner Aaron Harris, Wharton's Adam Grant, and Duke's Dan Ariely.
jobs.tailored_advice: Esta oferta busca perfiles senior con más de 4 años de experiencia. Tu perfil es muy prometedor pero junior. Para destacar, enfatiza en tu CV la arquitectura de tus proyectos, cómo manejas la escalabilidad y los retos técnicos específicos que superaste al integrar IA, en lugar de solo listar las tecnologías. Dado que A·Team busca 'constructores', resalta tu capacidad de tomar decisiones de producto, no solo de implementación.
jobs.cover_letter: Como desarrollador apasionado por la creación de productos desde cero, he seguido de cerca la propuesta de A·Team. Mi enfoque no se limita a escribir código, sino a construir soluciones completas que resuelven problemas reales, tal como he demostrado con mi plataforma 'El Grano de Oro', donde integré modelos de IA para mejorar la experiencia de usuario. Mi capacidad para gestionar el ciclo de vida completo del desarrollo, desde la arquitectura hasta el despliegue, se alinea con la autonomía que buscan en sus misiones de '0 a 1'. Aunque mi trayectoria es joven, mi enfoque autodidacta y mi experiencia técnica con JavaScript, Docker y la integración de APIs de IA me han permitido lanzar proyectos en producción que ya generan valor. Estoy muy interesado en la posibilidad de colaborar en equipos ágiles y de alto impacto, aportando mi capacidad técnica y mi mentalidad orientada a resultados. Agradezco la oportunidad de presentar mi perfil para formar parte de su red de constructores independientes.

jobs.title: Senior Quality Engineer (Campinas)
jobs.description:
This is a remote role for candidates located in Campinas, Brazil.

 

About LawnStarter

LawnStarter is the nation's leading on-demand marketplace for lawn care and outdoor services, with over $100M in annual bookings. We're expanding beyond lawn care to become the one-stop shop for all home services, operating across three brands (LawnStarter, Lawn Love, Home Gnome) on a single shared platform.

About Engineering at LawnStarter

We've spent the last two years rebuilding the platform from the ground up: new customer app, new Pro onboarding, job distribution engine, and data infrastructure. The foundation is solid. Now we're moving into expanded service verticals, dynamic pricing, and AI-powered features at scale. Quality needs to keep up.

Our stack is PHP (Laravel) and React/React Native, with Cypress as our primary test framework. We actively use Claude Code as part of our engineering workflow, not as an experiment, but as a core part of how we work.

 

The Role

As we scale into new service verticals and grow the engineering team, quality can't be reactive. This role exists to make quality proactive: owning the automation coverage, shaping the bug process, and partnering closely with Product, Design, and the Data team to catch problems before they reach customers.

You'll report to the Engineering Manager and be embedded across the engineering organization. This is hands-on and strategic, helping raise the quality ceiling for the whole team.

What makes this role different:

  • Cross-functional influence: You work directly with engineers, PMs, Designers, and the Data team to surface risks early, understand requirements deeply, and propose better solutions. You may be embedded in one or two delivery teams.
  • Automation ownership: You own the strategy for building and expanding our automated test coverage, not just executing test cases someone else wrote.
  • Process shaper: You'll identify gaps in our quality process and have the authority to fix them, not just flag them.

 

What You'll Own

  • Automated test coverage: Design and grow our E2E and integration test suite using Cypress. Set the strategy, prioritize coverage gaps, and raise the floor across the platform.
  • Bug process: Own the full lifecycle from triage to production fix. Keep it moving, keep stakeholders informed, and make sure nothing falls through the cracks.
  • Cross-functional quality partnership: Collaborate with PMs, Designers, and the Data team to review requirements, raise risks early, and define what "done" actually means.
  • Process improvement: Identify weaknesses in how we deliver software and propose solutions. Not just document them.
  • Exploratory testing: Test new features and existing flows on web and mobile across devices, catching what automation misses.

 

Problems to Solve

Keeping automation coverage sharp as the product evolves Our E2E coverage is solid. The challenge is staying ahead: bringing new ideas, exploring different types of coverage, and finding smarter ways to automate different aspects of the application as the product grows and new service verticals come online.

Exploring new tools and leveraging AI to improve quality operations AI is changing how we build software, and it's changing how we can test it too. There's real opportunity here to use AI tools to increase output quality, cut repetitive work, and discover coverage gaps we'd otherwise miss. We want someone who actively explores that space, not someone who waits to be told what to use.

 

What Success Looks Like (Year 1)

  • Automation coverage stays strong: New features ship with automated tests as a baseline, and coverage strategies evolve with the product.
  • Bug process runs smoothly: Triage to fix cycle is predictable and stakeholders know where things stand without having to ask.
  • Deep business knowledge: You understand how the marketplace works well enough to anticipate edge cases on your own.
  • Quality process improvement shipped: At least one meaningful improvement to how we deliver quality is in place and being used by the team.



Requirements

Who You Are

AI-native. You use AI tools like Claude Code to write, maintain, and extend test code, not as a novelty, but as a standard part of how you work. You're comfortable with browser MCPs, agent workflows, and building scripts that automate the boring parts of QA. This is unlikely to be a good fit if you're skeptical of AI in the engineering workflow.

Automation-first. Your default when you find a problem is to ask how to prevent it automatically. You build test infrastructure that outlasts any individual test case. This is unlikely to be a good fit if your strength is manual testing and automation is something you tolerate rather than drive.

Customer-obsessed. You think like a user. When you're testing a flow, you're asking whether it actually works for a real customer, not just whether it matches the spec. This is unlikely to be a good fit if you treat quality as a checkbox rather than a standard.

Adaptable. You don't get attached to a single tool, stack, or process. We're in a period of rapid evolution in how software gets built and tested, especially with AI, and you lean into that rather than resist it. This is unlikely to be a good fit if you thrive only in stable, well-defined tooling environments.

Collaborative and direct. You work across Product, Design, Engineering, and Operations. You surface risks early, speak plainly about what you find, and push back when something isn't ready to ship. This is unlikely to be a good fit if you prefer to work in isolation or soften feedback to avoid friction.

Technically solid. You're comfortable reading and writing code. You understand front and backend web services well enough to test them properly, write meaningful SQL queries, and debug issues across the stack. This is unlikely to be a good fit if your technical depth is limited to a single layer or framework.

 

This Role Is NOT

  • A manual-testing role: Most of your testing work should be building automation, not executing test scripts by hand.
  • A ticket-executor role: You're expected to shape what gets worked on and how, not just process a backlog.
  • A single-tool role: We use Cypress today, but we adapt. If you're only comfortable in one framework or one part of the stack, this will be limiting.
  • A role for someone who avoids ambiguity: The business is changing fast. New verticals, new flows, new edge cases. You need to be comfortable moving before everything is defined.
  • A back-of-the-line role: Quality is a first-class concern here. You're expected to have opinions and influence the process upstream, not just verify what's already been built.



Benefits

Compensation & Benefits

  • Base salary: $45-$65k
  • Equity: We want you invested in the quality and long-term health of the platform you're helping build.
  • Fully remote: Work from anywhere in the Americas. Deep focus and async collaboration are the norm here.
  • Unlimited PTO: We focus on results. Take what you need.

 

LawnStarter provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or genetics. We comply with applicable state and local laws governing nondiscrimination in employment.
jobs.tailored_advice: Highlight your hands-on experience with AI-assisted coding tools like Claude Code or GitHub Copilot, as this is a core requirement. Emphasize your ability to build scalable Cypress test suites rather than just manual testing. Since this is a Senior role, showcase specific examples where you influenced product requirements or improved the bug triage process to prevent issues upstream. Mention your proficiency in PHP/Laravel and SQL to demonstrate you can debug across the full stack, and frame your experience in terms of business impact (e.g., reducing production defects or increasing deployment velocity).
jobs.cover_letter:
LawnStarter’s mission to scale home services through a robust, AI-integrated platform resonates deeply with my own approach to software development. Having built full-stack applications—including a marketplace with AI-driven recommendations—I understand the importance of maintaining high quality while rapidly expanding features. My experience with PHP, Laravel, and React aligns perfectly with your current stack, and I am eager to bring my automation-first mindset to your engineering team.

I am particularly drawn to your proactive approach to quality. In my own projects, I prioritize building scalable test suites rather than just executing manual checks. I am highly proficient with Cypress for E2E testing and have integrated AI tools like Claude Code into my daily workflow to streamline development and identify edge cases early. I don't just write code; I build systems that ensure reliability from the database layer to the user interface.

I thrive in environments where I can influence the development process upstream. Whether it is debugging complex SQL queries or refining the bug triage lifecycle, I am comfortable working across teams to ensure that quality is a standard, not an afterthought. I am ready to help LawnStarter maintain its high standards as you enter new service verticals.

I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how my technical background and passion for AI-driven automation can contribute to the continued success of your platform. Are you available for a brief conversation next week?
